Context:
Aqua City, NovaWorld deliveries to support H2 2022 profit We reiterate our MARKET PERFORM rating for NVL and maintain our target price of VND75,000/share as a lower net debt balance at end-Q2 2022 is offset by our 0.5-ppt higher risk-free rate assumption. We forecast 2022F NPAT-MI of VND6.9tn (USD302mn; +115% YoY), which is mainly driven by our expectation for presold unit deliveries at the Aqua City and NovaWorld projects to accelerate in H2 2022F. We increase our 2022F NPAT-MI forecast by 24% mainly due to higher-than-expected H1 2022 financial income. We also note that revaluation gains upon the consolidation of investments (land banks/subsidiaries) and divestment gains in H1 2022 accounted for a total of ~25% of our 2022F PBT forecast. NVL reported a total contracted value of ~USD2.4bn (+26.6% YoY) in H1 2022 mainly from the Aqua City, NovaWorld Phan Thiet and NovaWorld Ho Tram projects. We maintain our 2022F presales forecast at VND76.4tn (USD3.3bn; -10% vs 2021’s high base) even though H1 2022 presales achieved 74% of our 2022F forecast as we believe presales at hospitality projects could be slower in H2 2022 amid the Government’s stricter credit supervision on speculative and high-end property products. Downside risk: Slower-than-expected cash collection from customers due to internal structure. Upside catalyst: Better-than-expected presales for key projects; earlier-than-expected launches of HCMC residential projects; additional project pipeline to justify higher valuation. Handovers of Aqua City and NovaWorld to support H2 2022 earnings. According to management, NVL’s end-Q2 2022 backlog was ~USD9.9bn (+54.7% YoY), which was mostly attributed by Aqua City with 50%, NovaWorld Phan Thiet with 24%, NovaWorld Ho Tram with 11% and HCMC residential projects with 15%. Management recently stated the construction progress of key projects is faster than its planned schedule, assuring that NVL will be on track for delivery in H2 2022. We forecast NVL’s 2022F NPAT-MI growth at 115% YoY. New project development plan in central Vietnam and HCMC. At an analyst meeting in August 2022, management stated that NVL expects to launch three new projects in late 2022 or early 2023, including (1) codeveloping one project in central Vietnam and the Grand Sentosa (formerly known as Kenton Node) project in Nha Be District, HCMC and (2) developing a new project in HCMC. Further details have not been disclosed. We have not factored these new project development plans into our forecasts and valuation for NVL. We continue to expect ongoing presales at current projects (mainly Aqua City, NovaWorld Phan Thiet and NovaWorld Ho Tram) have the most significant contributions to our presale forecasts of USD3.3bn in 2023F (flat YoY growth vs 2022F). H1 2022 Recap: Higher financial income supports YoY flat profit Figure 1: H1 2022 audited results Source: Company’s financial statements, VCSC forecast 2022 Outlook: Handovers of NovaWorld Phan Thiet, Ho Tram & Aqua City to drive H2 2022F earnings Figure 2: 2022 forecast Source: Company’s financial statements, VCSC forecast Valuation We continue to use the RNAV valuation method and derive a one-year target price of VND75,000/share. The RNAV valuation method is best suited to capture the company’s long-term growth potential, in our view. We have factored in all identified land bank for projects that are under construction and under development via the DCF method of discounting estimated FCF to mid-2022. In early June 2022, NVL announced that it issued a total bond amount of VND5.77tn (USD251mn) to Warburg Pincus in order to fund NVL’s project development. They are 10-year bonds (maturity in 2032) that include (1) VND5.54tn (USD241mn) of convertible bonds — unsecured and without any warrant — with a fixed coupon rate of 10% per annum and

Context:
9M results beat expectation on strong oil price GAS released robust Q3 results with NPAT jumping by 68.4% YoY to VND3.2tn (USD137.4mn) and 9M NPAT growing by 50.3% YoY to VND8.9tn (USD381mn). The main reasons were the strong oil price and solid volume growth apart from VND500bn (USD21.4mn) of abnormal profit from the condensate segment. 9M reported and recurring NPAT accounts for 77.1% and 72.8% of our 2018 forecasts, respectively. As Q4 is peak season and with the current Brent oil price of USD80/bbl compared to our average 2018 assumption of USD75/bbl, we see 5%-10% upside risk to our 2018 recurring earnings forecast. We currently forecast 18.1% growth in 2019 earnings given 3.0% volume growth, the new Wild Orchid gas field coming online in early 2019 and the full operation of Ca Mau GPP. Strong oil price led to good gas and LPG prices. In 9M 2018, the average Brent price was USD72.0/bbl, up 46.9% YoY, which resulted in an increase in gas prices across the power plant, industrial park and LPG segments. In particular, the fuel oil price (which is the benchmark for most output gas prices) rose 33.4% YoY to USD407.3/ton and LPG soared by 37.5% YoY. Dry gas sales volume also resilient. Q3 sales volume growth was robust at 7.2% YoY, leading to 9M sales volume growth of 2.6% YoY. Condensate sales volume increased abnormally by 170% YoY. According to the company, there was some volume that was not booked in the previous two years that, after finalizing procedures, can now get revenue of VND500bn (USD21.4mn). We are seeking details on this. Ca Mau Gas processing plant (GPP) is operating very well. Ca Mau GPP generated VND480bn (~USD20.6mn) of NPAT in 9M, which meets our full-year forecast. We are currently expecting that this plant will run at 100% utilization rate (compared to 80% in 2018) and could generate profit of VND1.2tn (USD51.5mn) in 2019. Financial expense soared on interest expense of the Ca Mau gas processing plant (GPP) and forex loss. Interest expenses rose by 57.4% to VND340bn (USD14.6mn) while GAS suffered a forex loss of VND188bn (USD8.0mn). Figure 1: GAS's 9M 2018 result Source: GAS & VCSC VCSC Rating and

Context:
Triển vọng phục hồi giai đoạn 2024-25 đã được phản ánh đầy đủ vào giá cổ phiếu Chúng tôi nâng giá mục tiêu thêm 15% nhưng duy trì khuyến nghị PHÙ HỢP THỊ TRƯỜNG cho CTCP Thế Giới Số (DGW) do giá cổ phiếu đã tăng 28% trong 3 tháng qua. Chúng tôi điều chỉnh tăng nâng giá mục tiêu chủ yếu do chúng tôi nâng dự báo tổng LNST sau lợi ích CĐTS giai đoạn 2024-2028 thêm 18%. Trong khi chúng tôi chỉ điều chỉnh tăng dự báo LNST sau lợi ích CĐTS năm 2024 thêm 4%, chúng tôi nâng dự phóng LNST sau lợi ích CĐTS các năm 2025/2026/2027/2028 lần lượt thêm 16%/21%/21%/21%. Chúng tôi cho rằng việc DGW mở rộng nhanh hơn dự kiến sang các danh mục ngoài CNTT (cụ thể là hàng tiêu dùng, thiết bị gia dụng thông minh và thiết bị văn phòng) trong giai đoạn 2022-2023 sẽ mang lại lợi nhuận cao hơn dự kiến trong trung và dài hạn. Do đó, chúng tôi nâng tổng doanh thu ngoài CNTT giai đoạn 2024-2028 thêm 43%, dẫn đến tổng lợi nhuận gộp tăng 29% trong kỳ. Trong báo cáo cập nhật này, chúng tôi đã bắt đầu đưa các dự báo cho Achison (công ty con hàng tiêu dùng công nghiệp được hợp nhất vào năm 2023) vào mảng thiết bị văn phòng của DGW và các sản phẩm mới thuộc danh mục ngoài CNTT mà DGW mở bán vào cuối năm 2023 - đầu năm 2024, ví dụ: đồ gia dụng Xiaomi, Chăm sóc cá nhân & gia đình Mitsuei, thiết bị văn phòng Poly, v.v. Với chuyên môn phân phối mạnh mẽ của DGW, cùng với sự gia tăng của các thương hiệu quốc tế tham gia thị trường Việt Nam, chúng tôi kỳ vọng doanh thu mảng ngoài CNTT của DGW sẽ tăng trưởng với tốc độ tăng trưởng kép hàng năm (CAGR) giai đoạn 2023-2028 là 19% (so với mức 10% trong dự báo trước đây) và đóng góp lần lượt 36%/63% vào doanh thu/lợi nhuận gộp của DGW trong năm 2028. Q4 core profits beat our forecast; cash balance surges GMD released Q4 2022 results with revenue increasing 2.7% YoY to VND1.1tn (USD46mn) and NPAT-MI that was flat YoY at VND189bn (USD7.9mn). The profits in Q4 2022 were mainly driven by GMD’s core businesses and offset by a YoY surge in net other losses and financial expenses in the quarter. At end-2022, GMD’s net debt balance plunged to VND666bn (USD28mn) vs VND1.2tn (USD50mn) at end-Q3 2022 mainly thanks to an increased cash balance in Q4 2022 as GMD received cash deposits (other short-term payables) from its partner(s) in the quarter. At end-2022, GMD’s other short-term payable balance surged to VND1.3tn (USD54mn) vs VND330bn (USD14mn) at end-Q3 2022. In Q4 2022, GMD’s revenue was up 7% QoQ, but its NPAT-MI declined 23% QoQ, which was mainly due to net other losses of VND65bn (USD2.7mn) vs marginal net other gains/(losses) in Q3 2022. We believe net other losses were mainly derived from GMD’s non-core rubber plantations. In 2022, GMD’s revenue increased 22% YoY to VND3.9tn (USD163mn) and its NPAT-MI surged 63% YoY to VND995bn (USD41mn), which were primarily boosted by GMD’s core businesses and net income from affiliates. GMD’s 2022 revenue and NPAT-MI completed 104% and 108% of our respective full-year forecasts mainly due to higher-than-expected operating profit and were partially offset by higher-than-expected net other losses and lower-than-expected net income from affiliates. Net income from affiliates trailed our forecasts in 2022, which we mainly attribute to weaker-than-expected profits from the Gemalink deep-sea port amid softened trade activities in Q4 2022 in southern Vietnam. We estimate Gemalink made a profit of VND122bn (USD5.1mn) vs our forecast of VND217bn (USD9mn) in 2022. In late 2022, GMD announced that it will divest its entire stake in the Nam Hai Dinh Vu (NHDV) port in Hai Phong. We therefore see potential upside risk to our 2023F profit forecasts if there is a successful divestment from the NHDV port at premium valuation, pending a fuller review. Q2 loss mainly due to retail store closures AST’s posted Q2 2020 results with revenue of VND46bn/USD2mn (-84.4% YoY) and NPAT-MI of -VND13bn/USD560,000 (-124.4% YoY). AST’s H1 2020 revenue was VND242bn/USD10mn (-55.5% YoY), completing 34.1% of our 2020 forecast, while its NPAT-MI was VND500mn/USD22,000 (-99.5% YoY), completing 3.6% of our 2020 forecast. We mainly attribute the negative results of AST in H1 2020 to the drop in its airport retailing business that has struggled due to the impact of COVID-19. AST’s other businesses — hotel and air catering — were also hit by the pandemic. As AST’s H1 2020 NPAT-MI is tracking behind of our 2020 forecast, we see downside risk to our full-year forecast, pending a more extensive review. We believe in an ultimate recovery of aviation sector as it has exhibited strong rebounds after past epidemics and economic shocks. However, AST is not our favorite stock in the aviation sector at the moment, primarily because of the uncertain pathway for recovery. COVID-19 hit all business segments — especially airport retailing. Following the restrictions on both domestic and international flights from early April to mid-May, AST closed all of its retail stores in seven airports in Vietnam. From mid-May to June, AST gradually resumed operating stores in domestic terminals. At the end of June, 85% of domestic stores (37 stores) were reopened, but all stores in international terminals remained closed. AST’s hotel was closed when flights were restricted and only resumed operation in early July. AST’s air catering associate VINACS also incurred losses in Q2 2020, which is reflected in AST’s VND6bn loss from associates vs a VND1bn gain in Q2 2019. Cost cutting supported the bottom line. Although AST incurred losses in Q2 2020, we have observed that the company has made efforts to cut costs, especially labor, outsourcing and leasing costs that dropped by 68%/77%/74% YoY, respectively, in Q2 2020. In terms of leasing costs, AST has received support from terminal operators. The operators of the Da Nang and Cam Ranh airports have waived the minimum guaranteed lease payment for AST. In addition, ACV has reduced 90% and 15%-25% of leasing fees (vs pre-COVID-19 levels) for closed stores and reopened stores, respectively. AST’s Q2 2020 and H1 2020 results Source: AST, VCSC

Context:
Cementing leadership with investments in technology We attended PNJ’s AGM on April 20, 2019. Management reaffirmed that jewelry retailing will remain PNJ’s primary growth driver in the foreseeable future. PNJ targets 23% NPAT growth in 2019 backed by a 29% jump in gold jewelry retail sales, which, in turn, will be driven by 18% SSSG and 40 new Gold stores. PNJ delivered stellar Q1 2019 results with reported NPAT jumping 28% YoY. We discussed these results in our Earnings Flash “Strong growth driven by jewelry retail” published on April 19, 2019. Shareholders approved to raise FY2018 cash dividend by VND200/share to VND2,000/share and a 3:1 bonus issuance (three existing shares get one new share). For FY2019, the BOD proposes VND2,400/share based on the current share count (or VND1,800 post-bonus issuance). The AGM approved a 1% ESOP for FY2018, to be issued in 2019, with an exercise price of VND26,600 based on the current share count (or VND20,000 post-bonus issuance). The lock-up period is three years with 30%, 30% and 40% of the shares to be unlocked after the first, second and third year from the date of issuance. The AGM also agreed to issue 400,000 new shares to Mr. Robert Willet, an independent board member, which will be executed after the above bonus issuance. The issuing price will be at a 25% discount to the average closing price of PNJ shares during the ten trading days leading up to the date of the BOD’s approval on the list of employees eligible for the aforementioned ESOP program. 100% of Mr. Willet’s shares will be unlocked after one year of the issuance. PNJ’s head of accounting and finance, Madam Lai, was elected as a new board member to replace a former board member who resigned in June 2018. New ERP system went live in late March 2019 with more optimizations to be added in the future. The new ERP system allows PNJ to record real-time data of each operational activity, including procurement, manufacturing, logistics and retailing, which will help to identify and improve inefficiencies. PNJ expects the new system to start contributing to the company’s financial performance from Q4 2019. Management also plans to add a customer relationship management (CRM) tool in 2019, which will help PNJ to enhance its sales projections based on customer data. PNJ targets watch retail sales to soar five times to VND130bn (USD5.6mn) in 2019, which tracks ahead of our current projection of VND112bn (USD4.9mn). Sales growth will be primarily driven by point-of-sale (POS) expansion, which will be concentrated in H2 2019 and focus on the shop-in-shop format, per PNJ. In Q1 2019, PNJ opened four Watch POS vs our full-year assumption of 17 POS. New management compensation scheme will include up to 1.5% ESOP per annum from FY2019 onward. During the AGM, the chairwoman emphasized that human capital is PNJ’s most prized asset and ESOP is an effective way to help recruiting and retaining talent. PNJ’s new compensation scheme Source: PNJ PNJ’s 2019 guidance and VCSC’s 2019 forecasts Source: PNJ

Context:
Strong Q1 revenue growth; on-track expansion plans We attended PNJ’s AGM on April 16. Our key takeaways are as follows. Preliminary Q1 2022 revenue tracked slightly ahead of our forecast. In Q1 2022, revenue surged 41%-42% YoY and was VND10.2tn-10.3tn (USD444mn-447mn) — equivalent to 44% of our full-year revenue forecast. We note that in 2019 and 2021 (2020’s quarterly results were abnormally volatile due to COVID-19), Q1 revenue accounted for 28%-37% of PNJ’s full-year revenue. The Q1 2022 result implies revenue growth of 41%-44% in March 2022. Preliminary Q1 2022 NPAT have not yet been disclosed. Growth to be underpinned by further store openings and capacity expansion. PNJ targets to open 35 to 40 new stores in 2022, which will increase the total store count across formats to 376-381 by YE2022. In 2022-2025, there is a target to open 30 to 40 stores per year — with the potential to open 50 stores per year. For the company’s capacity expansion plan in 2022-2025, PNJ expects to add one to two gold jewelry product lines at its Long Hau plant (PNJ’s second factory in Long An Province). This plant currently specializes in silver and Italian-style jewelry. Additionally, the company is on track to obtain space for a new factory investment and expects to start investment within 2022. Per management, PNJ’s factories are currently operated at nearly 100% of design capacity of four million items per year. Management states that net profit from this capacity expansion, when completed, is expected to contribute 20% of PNJ’s net profit. Summary of key AGM resolutions Shareholders at the AGM approved 2022 guidance for net revenue of VND25.8tn (USD1.1bn; +32.2% YoY) and NPAT of VND1.3tn (USD57mn; +28.2% YoY), representing 111% and 95% of our respective forecasts. In 2016-2021, PNJ’s actual NPAT either followed or surpassed its guidance — except for 2021 due to the severe disruptions from COVID-19. Additionally, shareholders approved a FY2021 cash dividend of VND2,000/share — equivalent to a dividend yield of 1.7%. Shareholders also approved an expected FY2022 dividend of 20% on par value. Furthermore, shareholders approved a share bonus at a ratio 3:1, which implies every 100 existing shares will receive an additional 33 shares; this will be executed in 2022. Lastly, shareholders approved an ESOP based on 2022 NPAT results in the condition that 2022 NPAT growth equals or exceeds 18% with a maximum 2.0% of outstanding shares and a lock-up period of three years (after each of the first two years, 30% of ESOP shares are transferable; after the three years, 100% of ESOP shares are transferable).

H1 2016 results are positive but not a surprise CTG recently released financial statements for H1 2016 with decent profit growth and an increase in provisioning. H1 2016 results are in line with our forecasts and we do not see much material change to the target price. We reiterate our MARKETPERFORM rating. Decent profit growth achieved in H1 2016 and NIM was kept stable at 2.8% as in Q1 2016. PBT and NPAT grew 10.2% and 12.6% Y-o-Y, respectively, and reached 53% of our forecast. Interest income increased by 17.1% and non-interest income increased by 21.5%, but operating expense also rose at a similar pace of 23.4%, with all numbers being growth vs H1 2015. After strong profit growth in Q1, the accumulated H1 profit growth was lower as CTG has provisioned more. Non-interest income growth was slower in Q2, down by 31% vs Q2 2015, while Q1 growth was 98% vs Q1 2015. Loan growth was strong at 10.2% vs 9.3% for the same period last year and deposit growth was impressive at 16.6%. Despite a low CAR, CTG pushed loan growth in the first half as the bank used the cover of subordinate debt expansion, which amounted to VND13.4 trillion (USD600 million) compared to VND8 trillion (USD358 million) in FY15. Robust deposit growth will lower its historically high LDR as an 80% LDR cap has been enforced since July 1, 2016 after Cir.06 was issued. NPL slightly decreased from 0.96% in Q1 to 0.91%, but group 2 loans increased. We hold our view of increased NPLs for CTG given the movement of group 2 loans and future merger with PGBank. Provision expense recorded 18.7% growth Y-o-Y due to higher general provision and provision for held-to-maturities securities. As loans grew 22.3% in FY15 and 10.2% in H1 2016, general provision has tracked upward vs H1 2015, accordingly. Provision for held-to-maturity securities was VND2.76 trillion (USD123 million) vs VND1.39 trillion (USD62 million) in FY15, but the amount of held-to-maturity securities reduced, therefore, it can be said that CTG has actively provisioned for the VAMC bonds. There were few write-offs and possibly no new sales to VAMC in H1 2016 as CTG currently has low official NPL, but we expect more write-offs in the following quarters as NPL is forecast to increase. Đối mặt với nhiều thách thức Chúng tôi đã tham dự ĐHCĐ thường niên của CTCP Thép Nam Kim (NKG) cho năm tài chính 2017 diễn ra ngày 08/04/2018 tại tỉnh Bình Dương. Ban lãnh đạo công ty đặt kế hoạch tăng trưởng doanh thu 35% và LNST 6% trong năm 2018, phù hợp với dự báo của chúng tôi khi tăng trưởng sản lượng mạnh mẽ sẽ bị ảnh hưởng bởi biên LN thấp hơn trong bối cảnh cạnh tranh gay gắt và xu hướng giá HRC (thép cuộn cán nóng) đầu vào bất lợi. KQKD sơ bộ quý 1/2018 kém tích cực khi LNST giảm mạnh 36% so với quý 1/2017, chủ yếu được dẫn dắt bởi mức giảm biên LN từ mức cơ sở cao năm 2017. Kế hoạch phát hành riêng lẻ trị giá 300 tỷ đồng, dự định được thực hiện trong tháng 4/2018 – tháng 6/2019 nhằm tài trợ vốn lưu động và các dự án tiếp theo, sẽ tiếp tục pha loãng EPS. Hai dự án mở rộng chính đang trong kế hoạch thực hiện nhưng sẽ chưa đóng góp vào lợi nhuận trong ngắn hạn. Cổ tức tiền mặt 1.000 đồng/CP đã được thanh toán trong trong khi 40% cổ tức cổ phiếu (10 cổ phiếu hiện hữu nhận 4 cổ phiếu mới) còn lại cho năm 2017 sẽ được thực hiện trong năm 2018. Kế hoạch cổ tức 2018 là 50% (bằng cổ tức hoặc cổ phiếu) cũng được thông qua. Chúng tôi ghi nhận những áp lực điều chỉnh giám giá mục tiêu của chúng tôi từ giảm biên LN và pha loãng từ phát hành quyền mua vừa công bố. Cạnh tranh gia tăng gay gắt, rủi ro về chiến tranh thương mại và giá đầu vào bất lợi sẽ dẫn đến tăng trưởng lợi nhuận một chữ số. Ban lãnh đạo đặt kế hoạch doanh thu và LNST 2018 đạt lần lượt 17 nghìn tỷ đồng (+35% so với 2017) và 750 tỷ đồng (+6% so với 2017). Sự chênh lệch giữa tăng trưởng doanh thu và lợi nhuận đến từ biên LN gộp giảm khi NKG đối mặt với cạnh tranh gay gắt hơn cả trong nước và thị trường xuất khẩu cũng như chi phí HRC đầu vào gia tăng. Chi tiết về triển vọng và dự báo cho NKG năm 2018 có thể được xem thêm trong báo cáo cập nhật của chúng tôi. KQKD sơ bộ kém tích cực trong quý 1/2018 xác nhận tăng trưởng lợi nhuận đang giảm tốc. LNST sơ bộ quý 1/2018 của NKG ước đạt 100 tỷ đồng, giảm 36% so với quý 1/2017 và chỉ hoàn thành 13% kế hoạch cả năm 2018 của NKG và dự báo của chúng tôi, chủ yếu do giảm biên LN gộp. Dù quý 1 là mùa thấp điểm cho các nhà sản xuất thép, chúng tôi vẫn ghi nhận kết quả sơ bộ này thấp hơn dự phóng của chúng tôi, và do đó sẽ cập nhật dự báo sau khi báo cáo tài chính đầy đủ được công bố. Ban lãnh đạo xác nhận tác động không đáng kể từ việc Mỹ áp thuế nhập khẩu lên thép. NKG xuất khẩu 50-60% tổng sản lượng của công ty nhưng chỉ 8% được xuất khẩu sang Mỹ, do đó, sẽ chỉ chịu tác động khiêm tốn từ mức thuế này. Earnings recovery plays out as expected We maintain our TP of VND59,500 for FRT but downgrade our rating from BUY to MARKET PERFORM as the share price has rallied 20% in the past month. We believe the current share price has priced in FRT’s earnings recovery post-Q1 2019 on the back of stronger accessory sales and margins as well as better support from Apple. Looking ahead, we believe that further re-rating of FRT will be premised on the progress of its pharmacy (Long Chau) roll-out. Prelim 5M 2019 NPAT grew 7% YoY (vs flat NPAT YoY in Q1 2019), driven by online sales and accessories, which more than offset weakness in new iPhone sales. We forecast NPAT to advance 9% YoY in full-year 2019, as we expect H2 2019 NPAT growth to accelerate on the back of stronger support from Apple and further ramp-up in accessory sales. Prelim 5M 2019 NPAT inched up 7% YoY, in line with our expectation. Online sales, which soared 45% YoY in 5M 2019, spearheaded top line growth of 8%. Moreover, as FRT has finished clearing its old accessory inventory, accessory ASP improved in April-May 2019 vs Q1 2019. Accordingly, 5M 2019 accessory revenue jumped ~20% YoY vs only 3% YoY in Q1 2019. On the other hand, combined revenue from F.Friends and telco price subsidy (TPS) programs edged up 3% YoY in 5M 2019 as FRT is pausing its TPS expansion due to some difficulties working with telcos. We reiterate our view that iPhone GPM will rebound from H2 2019 as the market rebalances and Apple shores up support for retailers. As we discussed in our previous reports Share price correction overdone dated April 3, 2019, and Earnings set to improve from Q2 2019 dated May 14, 2019, Apple started to provide support for retailers in April 2019 to push sales of new iPhones. This partly bolstered FRT’s April-May 2019 revenue/NPAT to climb 16%/16% YoY, per our estimate, vs only 3%/1% YoY in Q1 2019. We maintain our assumption that Apple GPM will shrink by 2.5 ppts YoY in full-year 2019 before rebounding by 1.9 ppts YoY in 2020. New experiments in mobile business remain in small scale. In addition to partnerships with Nguyen Kim (the second leading consumer electronics chain in Vietnam) and Fado (a cross-border e-commerce player) to leverage on its existing online and offline platforms, FRT recently added postal and delivery services to its business lines. Per management, the addition of these businesses is part of FRT’s planned pilot of new in-store services in the near future; however, no further details have been disclosed at this point. Having said the above, in our opinion, contributions from these pilot projects will be insignificant in the medium term. Long Chau expansion is key for sustainable re-rating. Long Chau’s sales/store remain strong (~VND1.6bn/USD68,700 in Q1 2019, per FRT). However, we continue to monitor the pace of store openings, which has shown signs of picking up lately. Per FRT, Long Chau’s store count reaches nearly 40 by end-Q2 2019 vs 28 by end-Q1 2019. 2019F: We expect NPAT to strengthen vs Q1, driven by accessories and recovering iPhone margins Figure 1: VCSC’s 2019 forecasts Source: FRT, VCSC, (1) VCSC estimates Valuation We apply a 30% discount to our blended DCF/PER valuation for FRT’s mobile business. Context:
Competition and rising costs point to tough year ahead We downgrade HT1 to OUTPERFORM and cut our target price to VND14,700 per share as HT1’s ability to maintain positive top-line growth becomes uncertain. We cut our 2018F NPAT-MI by 29% to VND456bn (USD20.1mn), down 4% vs 2017, as we expect negative volume and ASP growth due to tougher competition as well as margin compression from rising input costs, which might only be partially passed through to customers. While capex is slowing down and financial leverage reducing, the positive benefit of cash flow generation will be more than offset by weaker earnings. A high cash dividend of VND1,500 per share (dividend yield of 12.0%) will be paid in Q2 or Q3 2018, but we do not consider this dividend level to be sustainable. Upside catalysts: recovery in sales volume, one-off profit from Thu Duc real estate project. Downside catalysts: higher electricity and coal prices, EUR/VND appreciation of more than 2%. Intensified competition pressures both HT1’s sales volume and prices. HT1’s Q1 2018 sales volume fell by 4.8% vs Q1 2017, underperforming the industry’s growth rate of 5.0%, due to intense competition between local producers. Per HT1’s management, the company is aggressively pushing sales from Q2 2018 going forward to recover volume growth. Nevertheless, we still expect competition will continue to pressure volume and ASP growth. Hence, we forecast a 2% decrease in cement sales volume and a 1% fall in ASP for 2018, translating to a 3% revenue decline for full-year 2018. Rising input costs to hit margins. HT1’s Q1 2018 gross margin dropped to 14.9% vs 15.9% in Q1 2017 as electricity and coal input prices rose by 6% and 10%, respectively, in Q1 2017. HT1 still continued to outsource cement production to the VICEM’s Ha Long grinding station, which further depressed its gross margin. Given HT1’s rising cost base, we lower our 2018 gross margin assumption to 14.5%. Pay-down of foreign currency denominated debt to lower interest burden and FX risks. We expect HT1 over 2018 to repay around VND580bn (USD25.7mn) of mostly EUR-denominated debt, equivalent to half of its 2017 ending gross debt balance. We expect the remainder of its debt balance to be extinguished in 2019, eliminating FX risks which have weighed on historical results. High cash dividend unlikely to be sustained without additional leverage. Although lower capex spending and lower leverage has improved HT1’s cash flow generation, a planned high cash dividend of VND572bn (USD25.2mn) to be paid in Q2 and Q3 this year will wipe away HT1’s cash balance, which stands at VND265.4bn (USD11.7mn) at the end of Q1 2018. We therefore do not consider this dividend stream to be sustainable and assume it will not be maintained in our forecasts. The company would need to raise debt to continue to fund dividends at this level. Thu Duc real estate project poses potential upside for earnings and stock price. As no details regarding this project have been disclosed, we continue to exclude it from our valuation. The strong demand recovery of the Middle East has caused a mass movement of JU rigs to the region, leading to rig shortage in other regions in the world — especially in Southeast Asia. Meanwhile, construction of drilling rigs remains quiet and there has been a minimal number of orders placed. PVD believes Indonesia, Malaysia and Thailand could suffer from rig shortages starting in Q2 2023 after these countries’ drilling campaigns are approved. In addition, these countries are cooperating to develop a series of drilling jobs to retain foreign drillers for long durations. In such a highly competitive environment for hiring rigs, PVD expects the Southeast Asian JU day rate to be USD120,000-140,000 at end-2023. As PVD has secured almost all of its contracts for 2023 in international markets (i.e., Indonesia, Malaysia, Thailand and Brunei), the drilling segment has a bright outlook for 2023 is bright. We expect this will be the main driver for PVD’s stronger performance in 2023. Quiet domestic drilling market poses downside risk to our forecast for the well technical services segment. Vietnam requires 1.5-2 rigs (excluding those of Vietsovpetro) working continuously due to three main reasons. Firstly, PetroVietnam (PVN) plans on carrying out small and scattered drilling campaigns in 2023. Secondly, 30%-40% of wells belong to Vietsovpetro and are drilled by its own rigs. Thirdly, drilling campaigns in Vietnam are mostly carried out in March to October due to favorable weather conditions, which causes high demand only during this period and low demand throughout the remainder of the year. Furthermore, domestic oil & gas operators are not willing to pay drillers high day rates. This situation is not favorable for PVD to work in Vietnam. However, job opportunities for well technical services in international markets are limited to foreign drillers like PVD due to the requirement for high localization rates and strict protection policies of the host countries. As a result, PVD expects the contribution from the well technical services segment to decrease in 2023. As we forecast PVD’s well technical services to increase 19% YoY in 2023, we foresee downside risk to our forecast for this segment, pending a fuller review. In addition, PVD is progressing to meet the requirements of host countries to be eligible to provide a bundle of services to its clients in these countries. We believe PVD’s well technical services in international markets will pick up after meeting the host countries’ requirements for localization rates and licenses. More domestic drilling campaigns to be carried out starting in 2024, supporting the recovery of drilling and well technical services segment. Management expects Vietnam’s revised Oil & Gas Law, which becomes effective on July 1, 2023, will facilitate exploration & production activities in Vietnam. There are some long-term domestic projects such as Block B, Dai Hung – Phase 3 (from July 2024), White Lion – Phase 2B, Yellow Camel (from Q3 2024) and Ca Tam that will progress further and provide jobs to the company. Management expects Block B to receive a final investment decision in Q3 2023, start conducting drilling campaigns at end-2025 and obtain its first gas in 2026. To benefit from the recovery of domestic oil & gas sector in 2024, PVD is prioritizing working in Vietnam and targeting to move two JU rigs back to Vietnam after finishing international contracts. Minimal risk of bad debt provision in 2023. PVD is working with related parties to collect bad debt from KrisEnergy (total of USD3.9mn). yếu hơn kỳ vọng. VEA – Tiếp cận với sự tăng trưởng dài hạn của thị trường ô tô và xe máy Việt Nam Tổng quan Công ty PNJ là nhà sản xuất và bán lẻ trang sức hàng đầu tại Việt Nam với mạng lưới 376 cửa hàng tính đến 5T 2023. Là công ty trang sức vàng thời trang giữ vị thế thống trị tại Việt Nam, PNJ đang được hưởng lợi từ sự tăng trưởng nhanh chóng của chi tiêu không thiết yếu tại Việt Nam. Vị thế dẫn đầu của công ty thậm chí còn được củng cố sau khi các đối thủ nhỏ hơn gặp khó khăn do COVID-19. Chúng tôi tin rằng cơ sở khách hàng trung lưu khá ổn định của PNJ — cùng với khả năng thiết kế, sản xuất và marketing vượt trội cũng như không ngừng đầu tư cho CNTT — sẽ giúp công ty nắm bắt được sự tăng trưởng nhu cầu trang sức thời trang của Việt Nam. Chúng tôi dự báo CAGR doanh thu bán lẻ đạt 11% trong giai đoạn 2023-27F. Sự cải thiện dự kiến của chúng tôi về danh mục sản phẩm và hiệu quả hoạt động của PNJ tiếp tục thúc đẩy dự báo CAGR EPS đạt 17% của chúng tôi đối với giai đoạn 2023-2025. Giá mục tiêu của chúng tôi tương ứng P/E năm 2023/2024 của PNJ lần lượt là 14x/11x, so với P/E trượt trung bình 5 năm là 17x và so với trung vị của P/E trượt trung bình 5 năm của các công ty cùng ngành trong khu vực là 25x. Yếu tố hỗ trợ: Nhu cầu cao hơn dự kiến đối với các sản phẩm cao cấp. Rủi ro đối với quan điểm tích cực của chúng tôi: Sự phục hồi yếu hơn dự kiến trong chi tiêu không thiết yếu; mở rộng cửa hàng chậm hơn dự kiến. PNJ – Doanh nghiệp dẫn đầu thị trường trang sức thời trang Việt Nam Tổng quan về công ty Viettel Construction là công ty con của Tập đoàn Viettel – công ty viễn thông số 1 Việt Nam. CTR cung cấp các dịch vụ xây dựng, vận hành, tích hợp hệ thống và cho thuê hạ tầng chủ yếu cho các nhà mạng viễn thông. CTR là công ty cho thuê hạ tầng viễn thông lớn nhất Việt Nam. CTR là nhà thầu và nhà vận hành cơ sở hạ tầng viễn thông hàng đầu Việt Nam, tự hào về khả năng cạnh tranh về chi phí đồng thời tận dụng hoạt động kinh doanh của công ty mẹ — công ty viễn thông số 1 Việt Nam Viettel. Chúng tôi tin rằng CTR sẽ tận dụng mức tiêu thụ dữ liệu di động ngày càng tăng của Việt Nam. Ngoài ra, các mảng xây dựng và tích hợp hệ thống của CTR đang phát triển nhanh chóng nhờ chuyên môn kỹ thuật vững chắc của công ty. Mảng towerco — qua đó CTR xây dựng các trạm viễn thông và cho các nhà khai thác mạng di động (MNO) thuê — ghi nhận doanh thu cốt lõi có biên lợi nhuận cao (ước tính biên EBITDA đạt 60% trở lên), tiềm năng tăng trưởng nhanh nhờ xu hướng thuê ngoài và chia sẻ cơ sở hạ tầng giữa các MNO và dư địa cho việc hợp nhất thị trường nhờ mỗi quan hệ tương hỗ giữa quy mô và chi phí. Chúng tôi dự kiến CAGR EBITDA đạt 21% cho giai đoạn 2022-25 - dẫn dắt bởi

Context:
Việc triển khai sản phẩm mới sẽ quyết định tương lai mảng sữa đậu nành Chúng tôi giữ khuyến nghị MUA dành cho CTCP Đường Quảng Ngãi (QNS) với giá mục tiêu gần như không đổi. Chúng tôi cho rằng cổ phiếu này đang bị định giá thấp tại mức PER 2018 là 8 lần khi 76% LNST được đóng góp bởi mảng sữa đậu nành có thị phần lớn nhất. Tuy nhiên, việc phân bổ vốn kém hiệu quả cho mảng đường đang ảnh hưởng đến việc tạo ra giá trị cho cổ đông. Chúng tôi dự báo ROIC (thu nhập trên vốn đầu tư) sẽ giảm dần từ mức đỉnh 36% năm 2015 xuống 19% năm 2020. LNST 6 tháng đầu năm 2018 tăng 15% dù doanh thu đi ngang, nhờ đóng góp của mảng điện sinh khối, sản lượng và khả năng sinh lời mảng đường được cải thiện, và biên lợi nhuận gộp mảng sữa đậu nành tăng. Chúng tôi dự báo LNST 2018 sẽ tăng trưởng 17% vì lợi nhuận mảng đường nhiều khả năng sẽ tiếp tục tăng mạnh trong 6 tháng cuối năm so với mức thấp cùng kỳ năm ngoái, khi bị ảnh hưởng bởi giá bán thấp trong khi chi phí sản xuất cao. Các sản phẩm mới được tung ra thị trường trong tháng 9 sẽ là yếu tố quyết định cho tăng trưởng dài hạn của mảng sữa đậu nành. Chúng tôi giữ thái độ thận trọng và dự báo EPS của QNS sẽ đạt tăng trưởng kép hàng năm 6% giai đoạn 2018-2020 Yếu tố hỗ trợ lợi nhuận: Các sản phẩm sữa đậu nành mới sẽ được tung ra trong Quý 3 có triển vọng tích cực, các biện pháp bảo hộ được ban hành có thể hỗ trợ giá đường trong nước. Doanh số bán sữa đậu nành sẽ cải thiện trong nửa cuối năm 2018, được hỗ trợ bởi sự phục hồi của tiêu thụ hàng tiêu dùng nhanh (FMCG) và sản phẩm mới vào cuối quý 3. Như đã đề cập trong báo cáo cập nhật VNM và GTN, chúng tôi kỳ vọng tiêu thụ sữa nói chung sẽ tăng tốc trong nửa cuối năm sau KQKD chung kém tích cực trong 6 tháng 2018. Với diễn biến này và kế hoạch triển khai sản phẩm mới của QNS vào cuối quý 3, chúng tôi dự báo doanh thu sữa đậu nành sẽ tăng 8% YoY trong 6 tháng cuối năm 2018 so với mức giảm 5% YoY trong 6 tháng đầu năm 2018, với giả định sản phẩm mới sẽ đóng góp 3% cho doanh thu cả 2018. Biên LN gộp mảng sữa đậu nành cải thiện nhờ chi phí đường và khấu hao giảm. Nhờ giá đường đầu vào và khấu hao giảm, chúng tôi dự phóng biên LN gộp mảng sữa đậu nành sẽ tăng 2,6 điểm % trong năm 2018 lên 42,2% và 1,3 điểm % lên 43,5% năm 2019. Tuy nhiên, biên LN từ HĐKD 2018 nhiều khả năng sẽ bị ảnh hưởng bởi hoạt động Quảng cáo & Khuyến mãi (A&P) mạnh hơn trong nửa cuối năm 2018, nhằm hỗ trợ cho việc triển khai sản phẩm mới. Do đó, chúng tôi chỉ dự phóng mức tăng 23 điểm cơ bản cho biên LN từ HĐKD của sữa đậu nành năm 2018. Mảng đường bắt đầu đóng góp lợi nhuận nhờ tăng trưởng sản lượng và chi phí sản xuất cải thiện. Chúng tôi dự báo sản lượng bán đường của QNS tăng mạnh 61% YoY trong năm

Context:
Bright prospects for phosphorus, chlor-alkali-vinyl We maintain our BUY rating and raise our target price by 18% as we lift our aggregate 2022F-2024F core EBITDA (ex-real estate) by 19%. In addition, we raise our target EV/EBITDA from 8.5x to 9x (applied on 2022F core EBITDA) to reflect DGC’s next phase of elevated growth starting from 2024 (2024F core EPS +33% YoY) after its chlor-alkali-vinyl (CAV) project comes online. Our aggregate EBITDA forecast increases due to our incorporation of (1) the second phase of the CAV project, (2) new capacity of thermal phosphoric acid (TPA) and (3) higher ASP projections for agricultural phosphates (APs). These factors are partly offset by higher input phosphate rock prices and our revised assumption that the first phase of the CAV project will commence operation in mid-2023 instead of early 2023 as previously. We expect rocketing phosphorus prices in Q3 2021 along with product mix improvements will buoy 2022F earnings. That said, we conservatively project an 18% correction in DGC’s 2023F phosphorus ASP as we think (1) China could partially ease extreme supply-side restrictions on phosphorus to meet rising domestic demand, (2) input prices could soften as the global supply chain stabilizes and (3) currently record high fertilizer prices could dampen demand. Risks to our positive view: Stronger-than-expected corrections in selling prices; changes in either domestic or foreign export tariffs on phosphorus that would hurt profit margins and competitiveness; potential environmental incidents that could disrupt operations; higher electricity prices in Vietnam (a major input cost); execution risks of the upcoming CAV project. Product price trends: Industrial phosphorus chemicals (IPCs) in line; APs better than expected. Recently, yellow phosphorus (P4) prices in China have corrected from USD9,200/tonne to USD5,600/tonne (vs USD2,500/tonne in H1 2021) as China implemented measures to boost its energy production. Meanwhile, Vietnam’s DAP fertilizer prices have surged 33% since end-Q3 2021 thanks to (1) strong demand, (2) soaring input costs such as phosphate rock, sulfur and ammonia, and (3) China and Russia’s export restrictions — key sources of imported fertilizer for Vietnam. We expect IPC prices to remain above 2020 levels amid a favorable demand-supply picture. Consulting firm Wood Mackenzie forecasts that global lithium iron phosphate production could grow fivefold to 750,000 tonnes in 2030 due to the electric vehicle battery industry. This amount contains 150,000 tonnes of P4, which is equivalent to ~13% of global P4 production in 2020, per our estimate. Meanwhile, we expect China’s policies on P4 production to remain restrictive — especially in the short term — given (1) its limited hydropower generation as the dry season lasts from December-May and (2) emission control ahead of the Beijing Olympics that will take place in February 2022. We assume the second phase of the CAV project will come online in 2024 and become a major medium-term driver. DGC is poised to become the largest producer of caustic soda and the first producer of PVC plastic feedstock in Vietnam with production capacities of 150,000 tonnes for each product. Vietnam faces a shortage of both products as it imported ~850,000 tonnes of PVC and PVC feedstock and 270,000 tonnes of caustic soda in 2020, per the International Trade Center. Meanwhile, as China reduces coal-based PVC production to reduce its carbon footprint, we expect PVC prices to stay above 2018-2020 levels despite a recent correction from their record high. 9M 2021: Robust results driven by strong demand for phosphorus chemicals, cost savings Figure 1: DGC's 9M 2021 results Source: DGC, VCSC; *9M 2021 revenue breakdown is VCSC’s estimate; we use phosphorus content as a proxy sales volume. LFP batteries emerges as major demand driver

Although it is trading in line with peers on a P/E basis, higher input gas costs and heavy capex needs will drag on earnings and cash flows over the next few years. Higher input costs pushed H1 2016 dry gas segment earnings below expectations but other segments outperformed. GAS’s H1 2016 consolidated revenue slid by 5% YoY while reported NPAT dropped by 42% YoY. The input gas price at Hai Thach – Moc Tinh gas field turned out to be ~20% higher than previously estimated, leading to a 5% increase in the average input price in H1. Dry gas segment’s gross profit plunged by 76% and accounted for 24% of our previous forecast. However, LPG segment (-14% YoY) and subsidiaries’ gross profit (-1% YoY) achieved 61% and 64% of our previous 2016 forecasts, respectively. Higher expected input gas prices going forward lead us to trim recurring earnings by ~6% across the forecast period. With the specific gas input price for Hai Thach – Moc Tinh being higher than expected, average gas input prices are likely to stay elevated. However, we also raise our average transportation tariff forecast by 2% given that the new Thien Ung-Dai Hung pipeline’s tariff was fixed at USD5/MMBTU vs our previous estimate of USD1/MMBTU. Huge capex on an LNG terminal and a pipeline will weigh on cash flows through 2020. GAS is planning to spend ~USD2.2 billion on Thi Vai LNG terminal, Nam Con Son 2 phase 2 and Block B – O Mon gas pipeline. These projects will drive earnings in the very long term, from 2021 onward but will eat into cash flow in the near term. Decommissioning charges on depleted fields are a risk to our valuation, but we do not have enough disclosure to model these. GAS stated that they have to book decommissioning expenses for gas pipelines upon the closure of gas fields at the end of their project lives in coming years, a first for the company. However, they cannot estimate the amount or timing. We estimate with limited confidence that this expense for may range from USD100 million-USD200 million, accounting for 4%-9% of GAS’s current forecast earnings over the next five to 10 years; if we assume the upper end of this range, our target price would decline another 3%. Our TP assumes oil will average ~USD48/bbl for H2 2016. Our current average oil price assumption of USD45/bbl for 2016 implies that the average oil price in H2 2016 will be ~USD48/bbl, 17% higher than in H1 2016. This should lift average selling prices across the power plant AToP, industrial park and LPG segments. However, our TP is sensitive to oil prices with the TSR ranging from -22.2% to -10.2% if we construct a range of -+10 USD/bbl around our current base case oil assumptions throughout the forecast period. This was mainly driven by gas output sold to power plants, which experienced a higher utilization due to the severe drought in the Central and the South regions denting hydropower production. Context:
Earnings slightly beat thanks to lower G&A expenses MWG announced its 2021 results, including revenue of VND123tn (USD5.3bn; +13% YoY) and NPAT-MI of VND4.9tn (USD213mn; +25% YoY). These results imply revenue of VND36tn (USD1.6bn; +33% YoY) and NPAT-MI of VND1.6tn (USD68mn; +66% YoY) in Q4 2021. 2021 revenue and NPAT-MI achieved 101% and 103% of our respective forecasts. We attribute the slight outperformance of MWG’s NPAT-MI vs our forecast to lower-than-expected G&A — particularly at BHX — as part of BHX’s cost optimization efforts amid weak sales in Q4 2021. Despite a 25% YoY slump in Q3 2021 due to strict social distancing restrictions, TGDD and DMX’s aggregate revenue advanced 9% YoY in 2021 thanks to (1) a continued rollout of super-mini-DMX stores (120-150 sqm/store) whose revenue reached VND6.8tn (USD296mn; +8x YoY) and contributed more than 7% to TGDD and DMX’s combined revenue in 2021, (2) a 17% YoY jump in mobile phone revenue that was led by iPhone sales, and (3) a 58% YoY surge in laptop revenue to VND5.5tn (USD239mn) thanks to remote working/studying demand. BHX’s revenue reached VND28tn (USD1.2bn; +33% YoY) in 2021, which was primarily driven by store expansion. BHX recorded positive quarterly EBITDA throughout Q2-Q4 2021 and we estimate its net margin improved from -9% in 2020 to -5% in 2021. We attribute this margin improvement mainly to a GPM expansion of approximately 3 ppts YoY in 2021 (per our estimate) that was bolstered by negotiations with suppliers as well as abnormally strong consumer demand in Q3 2021. An Khang (pharmacy chain) turned net profitable in late 2021 as its store count increased from 68 at YE2020 to 178 at YE2021. In Q4 2021, MWG raised its ownership in An Khang from 49% to 100%. The company plans to allocate more labor and financial resources to this business to accelerate its expansion. Online sales soared 53% YoY to VND14.4tn (USD625mn) in 2021 — of which TGDD and DMX’s combined online sales surged 47% YoY to VND13.4tn (USD583mn; ~14% of TGDD and DMX’s total revenue) while BHX’s online sales surged ~260% YoY to ~VND965bn (USD42mn; 3.4% of BHX’s total revenue). A 176% YoY surge in net financial income to VND552bn (USD24mn) further elevated MWG’s bottom line. Apple authorized reseller chain TopZone delivered upbeat early results. TopZone’s store count reached 10 as of YE2021 and its average monthly sales/store reached VND15-20bn (USD650,000-870,000) in Q4 2021. Given these positive results, management targets that TopZone will reach a total store count of 200 at YE2022 (vs our current projection of 87) and generate revenue of VND5tn (USD217mn) in 2022 (vs our current forecast of USD103mn). Cost optimization cushioned BHX’s margins amid slumping sales. Per our estimate, BHX’s monthly sales/store fell to ~VND950mn (USD41,000) in Q4 2021 vs VND1.2bn-1.6bn (USD54,000-70,000) in Q1-Q3 2021. Management attributes this weakness to (1) blue-collar workers (i.e., BHX’s customers) returning to their hometowns from big industrial cities following the lift of mobility restrictions — these cities typically account for nearly 50% of BHX’s revenue — and (2) renewed competition as alternative retail channels such as wet markets, mom-and-pop shops and on-premises outlets reopened. Nevertheless, thanks to a wider GPM and cost optimization, we estimate BHX’s net margin improved by 3.2 ppts YoY to -6.8% in Q4 2021. MWG’s 2021 results Source: MWG, VCSC; *TGDD figures include TopZone

Ngoài ra, chúng tôi kỳ vọng 1) PTB sẽ tiếp tục các đơn hàng đá có biên lợi nhuận thấp từ năm 2025 trở đi trong bối cảnh cạnh tranh gay gắt trong mảng kinh doanh có biên lợi nhuận cao và 2) tiến độ bàn giao các dự án bất động sản (BĐS) sẽ trong giai đoạn 2025-28 (thay vì giai đoạn 2025-27 như trước đây). Tuy nhiên, dự báo LNST sau lợi ích CĐTS mới của chúng tôi vẫn cho thấy mức tăng trưởng mạnh, lần lượt ở mức 27%/13%/13% trong các năm 2026/27/28. Chúng tôi dự báo PTB sẽ trích lập dự phòng 10 tỷ đồng còn lại của khoản trích nợ xấu liên quan đến khách hàng Noble House trong năm 2025. Giá mục tiêu của chúng tôi tương ứng với P/E dự phóng các năm 2025/26 lần lượt là 13,8 lần/10,8 lần. Chúng tôi cho rằng PTB xứng đáng với mức định giá cao hơn so với mức P/E trượt trung bình 5 năm là 9,7 lần nhờ mức tăng trưởng EPS mạnh mẽ 27% YoY dự kiến trong năm 2025. Rủi ro: Hoạt động xây dựng nhà ở và tiêu dùng tại Mỹ yếu hơn kỳ vọng; các thay đổi trong chính sách thương mại có khả năng ảnh hưởng tiêu cực đến kim ngạch xuất khẩu của Việt Nam. Thuế quan của Mỹ có tác động tích cực đến xuất khẩu đồ gỗ nội thất của Việt Nam. Mỹ hiện đang áp dụng mức thuế 20% đối với hàng nhập khẩu từ Trung Quốc và 25% đối với hàng nhập khẩu từ Mexico và Canada, có hiệu lực từ ngày 04/03/2025. The West-East pipeline is being considered to transport gas between south-western and south-eastern Vietnam to help balance demand and supply between East and West Vietnam. OCB must deliver on NFI to sustain currently high ROE OCB is slated to list on HOSE on January 28, 2021, at a reference price of VND22,900/share. OCB is smaller than all of the banks in our coverage universe by asset size with total assets of VND133tn/USD5.7bn vs VND193tn/USD8.3bn for current last-place TPB as of Q3 2020. OCB’s loan book composition is most like HDB because of the high concentration in retail and the smaller end of small to medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), accounting for 33% and 41% of its Q3 2020 loan book, respectively. The bank achieved 2020 preliminary PBT of VND4.4tn (+36.8% YoY), completing 100% of its 2020 AGM target. FY2020 ROE and ROA were 24.4% and 2.60%, respectively. Management targets to triple the bank’s 2019 total assets by 2025 with expected annual credit and deposit growth of 20%. ROE and ROA are targeted for at least 20% and 2%, respectively. On paper, OCB seems to have overcome its small scale to deliver one of the highest ROEs relative to our coverage universe; however, this has been highly dependent on Government bond trading since 2018. OCB’s annualized ROE drops by 650 bps to 13.1% using 9M 2020 results excluding gains from investment securities, bringing its adjusted ROE ranking just above BID and STB. As such, the VCSC house view that Government bond yields will rebound slightly in 2021 does not bode well for the continuation of these outsized trading gains at OCB. Current deposit structure challenges OCB’s ability to increase the competitiveness of its cost of funds. Lending to the smaller end of SMEs and retail has secured high IEA yields for OCB. However, OCB’s deposit dependence on SMEs and corporates (41.8% of its total deposits) elevates its cost of funds given that OCB has a less affluent SME base than ACB to enhance its CASA deposits (OCB’s CASA of 11.4% vs peer median 17.3% as of Q3 2020). As management targets to grow assets by 21% annually from 2021-2025, we expect the reliance on corporate deposits and valuable papers will continue. NFI has plenty of room for growth. The bank recorded 10.4% pure NFI/TOI as of 9M 2020 — a level similar to the peer median. However, with continuing investments in fintech and an exclusive collaboration with Generali to distribute bancassurance since 2019, we believe NFI is a bright spot. NPL level subpar given major exposure to the smaller end of SMEs while all VAMC has been cleared. OCB’s Q3 2020 NPL ratio was 2.15%, trending up from 1.84% at YE2019 and ranking as second highest ratio compared to our coverage in Q3 2020 — only behind VPB. As its listing price is close to private bank peer multiples, the upside for OCB is a bet that Vietnam’s macro backdrop will support an inherently riskier weighting in SME lending and OCB’s ample capital buffer to pursue growth. At a listing price of VND22,900/share, OCB is trading at a trailing-12-month P/E and current P/B of 8.3x and 1.60x, respectively. Benchmarking with our coverage universe valuation, OCB is trading closely to the median peer TTM P/E and current P/B of 9.0x and 1.64x, respectively, based on closing prices on January 20, 2021. Context:
Financial income rescues ACV’s bottom line in 2021 ACV posted Q4 2021 results with revenue of VND960bn (USD41mn; -43% YoY) and NPAT-MI of VND330bn (USD14mn; +19% YoY). The divergence between the top and bottom-line growth was thanks to a 74% YoY surge in financial income in Q4 2021 that was mainly derived from a foreign exchange revaluation gain of VND524bn (USD23mn), accounting for 55% of total financial income in the quarter. We note that the revenue and NPAT-MI YoY comparison in Q4 2021 and full-year 2021 was also distorted by contributions from State-invested aviation assets, which started from 2021. Excluding contributions from State-invested aviation assets (such as runways), ACV’s revenue plunged 59% YoY to VND687bn (USD30mn) in Q4 2021 while its NPAT-MI dropped 28% YoY VND200bn (USD8.6mn). In 2021, ACV’s revenue dropped 39% YoY to VND4.8tn (USD205mn) while its NPAT-MI plunged 50% YoY to VND830bn (USD36mn). Excluding contributions from State-invested aviation assets, ACV’s 2021 revenue dropped 53% YoY to VND3.6tn (USD155mn) while its NPAT-MI plunged 70% YoY to VND499bn (USD22mn). We believe ACV’s weak profit in 2021 was mainly due to (1) a relatively high base in Q1 2020 when Vietnam had not yet implemented restrictions on international air transportation and (2) the severe negative impact from COVID-19 on Vietnam’s domestic market in Q3 2021. ACV’s 2021 revenue and NPAT-MI (excluding contributions from State-invested assets) completed 72% and 86% of our respective full-year forecasts. Additionally, we believe international flights remained limited despite Vietnam implementing a pilot program in November 2021 to welcome international arrivals. Therefore, we see potential downside risk to our 2021F forecasts for ACV — pending a fuller review. Financial income was ACV’s main profit driver. In 2021, ACV recognized VND3.3tn (USD141mn; +46% YoY) of financial income, including interest income of VND1.7tn (USD74mn; -19% YoY) and foreign exchange revaluation gains of VND1.4tn (USD60mn) vs only VND8.6bn (USD370,000) in 2020. The revaluation gains were due to JPY depreciation vs the VND as ACV had a long-term debt balance of JYP69bn as of end-2021. G&A expenses surged in 2021 due to provision for doubtful receivables. In 2021, ACV’s G&A expenses were up 98% YoY to VND1.3tn (USD57mn), including VND545bn (USD24mn) of provision expenses. Excluding these expenses, ACV’s G&A expenses were up 16% YoY in 2021. At end-2021, the doubtful receivable balance surged to VND2.1tn (USD91mn) from the end-2020 balance of only VND125bn (USD5.4mn). These new doubtful receivables are from Vietnamese airlines, including Pacific Airlines, VietJet Air, Bamboo Airways and Vietnam Airlines. In its balance sheet as of end-2021, ACV recorded a provision balance for doubtful receivables of VND604bn (USD26mn), accounting for 29% of its total doubtful receivable balance. Figure 1: ACV’s Q4 and 2021 results Source: ACV, VCSC. Note: (*) Excluding contribution from State-invested aviation assets; (**) NPAT-MI from assets owned by the State (State-invested aviation assets) — the State authorizes ACV to manage these assets (including runways).

Context:
M&C and FPSO segments trail expectation PVS released Q1 2020 results with revenue and NPAT-MI declining 20.8% YoY and 69.8% YoY, respectively, due to 1) slow construction progress of the mechanics & construction (M&C) segment on the Sao Vang – Dai Nguyet (SV-DN) projects due to the COVID-19 pandemic and 2) profit from floating oil storage (FPSO/FSOs) joint ventures dropping 82.2% YoY. Q1 revenue, reported NPAT-MI and recurring NPAT-MI completed 18.8%, 9.9% and 11.8% of our respective full-year forecasts. We note that Q1 2019 was a high base as it contributed ~50% of 2019 NPAT-MI, which was thanks to a high workload from the SV-DN projects. However, these Q1 2020 results trailed our expectation due to the M&C and FPSO segments. We foresee downside risk to our 2020F forecasts, pending for a fuller review. M&C segment trailed expectations mainly due to slow progress of SV-DN projects. Q1 M&C revenue and gross profit dropped 46.8% YoY and 74.6% YoY, completing 15.7% and 12.3% of our full-year forecasts, respectively. We attribute the poor M&C performance to the slowed construction progress of the SV-DN projects (offshore installation and collecting pipeline) due the negative impact of COVID-19. As a result, the projects might come online in H1 2021 instead of October 2020 as previously expected. In addition, we believe the COVID-19 pandemic has negatively affected progress of the Gallaf project in Qatar. Income from FSO/FPSO joint ventures plunged 82.2% YoY and accounted for only 4.9% of our forecast. In March 2020, PVS announced that it signed a contract extension for the Lam Son FPSO. The contract length is four years (from July 2017 to July 2021); however, there is a term in the contract that states both parties can terminate the contract with a 90-day notice. Therefore, we suppose this segment’s poor Q1 performance segment could be due to possible provision expenses related to the FPSO Lam Son as a result of the above term. We will investigate this further and revise our forecast accordingly. Low oil prices could slow progress of oil & gas projects. As stated in our March 24, 2020 Energy Sector Update, lower oil prices would negatively affect PVS as they could lower service prices and result in fewer newly-signed contracts. We already expect the Blue Whale project will be delayed for one year and start in 2021 while Block B will be delayed for two years with construction starting in 2024F. In addition, we have already applied a 30% discount on fair value to PVS given wide oil price fluctuations and the current weak sentiment for oil & gas stocks. Figure 1: PVS Q1 2020 results Source: PVS, VCSC estimates. Note: Recurring NPAT-MI excludes the impact of 1) gain/loss from asset revaluation related to the dissolution process of the seismic survey segment, 2) gain/loss from asset revaluation of changing the lease type of FSO/FPSOs and 3) provisions for bad debts related to the Long Phu 1 project. Meanwhile, we treat the expenses/profit from M&C guaranteed provisions/reversal as a recurring item as this is recurring activity for any construction.

Giá mục tiêu thấp hơn của chúng tôi đến từ 1) mức giảm 53% của chúng tôi đối với dự báo LNST sau lợi ích CĐTS cốt lõi năm 2022, tương ứng với dự báo lợi nhuận thấp hơn cho đội giàn khoan tự nâng (JU), với giả định chi phí hoạt động cao hơn và 2) dự báo lãi suất phi rủi ro cao hơn 50 điểm cơ bản. Trong khi đó, chúng tôi gần như giữ nguyên dự báo LNST sau lợi ích CĐTS giai đoạn 2023-2026. Sự phục hồi gần đây của thị trường JU Đông Nam Á và hiệu suất hoạt động khiến chúng tôi tăng dự báo giá thuê ngày trung bình của giàn JU trong giai đoạn 2023-2026 cho PVD. Tuy nhiên, theo kết quả kinh doanh 6 tháng đầu năm 2022 thấp, chúng tôi dự báo chi phí hoạt động cho các hợp đồng khoan quốc tế của PVD sẽ cao hơn và từ đó làm tăng chi phí tiền mặt hoạt động trung bình trong giai đoạn 2023-2026. Do đó, chúng tôi gần như giữ nguyên dự báo LNST sau lợi ích CĐTS giai đoạn 2023-2026. Dự báo LNST sau lợi ích CĐTS cốt lõi điều chỉnh cho năm 2022 của chúng tôi là 3,9 triệu USD vẫn cho thấy sự phục hồi đáng kể so với LNST sau lợi ích CĐTS là 1,8 triệu USD vào năm 2021, được thúc đẩy bởi giá thuê ngày của giàn JU tăng 14,2% YoY, mảng dịch vụ kỹ thuật giếng khoan phục hồi và đóng góp cả năm của giàn TAD. Chúng tôi kỳ vọng sự phục hồi mạnh mẽ vào năm 2023 với LNST sau lợi ích CĐTS cốt lõi tăng 6,6 lần YoY do giá thuê ngày của giàn JU tăng 15% YoY và đội giàn khoan hoạt động gần 100% công suất. Tuy nhiên, định giá của PVD hợp lý với P/E dự phóng 2023 là 25,7 lần và P/B quý gần nhất là 0,9 lần so với ROE dự kiến dưới 10% trong 3 năm tới. Ngoài ra, P/B của PVD cao gấp đôi so với mức trung vị P/B quý gần nhất của các công ty cùng ngành trong khu vực là 0,4 lần. Yếu tố hỗ trợ: Thị trường khoan dầu Đông Nam Á phục hồi nhanh hơn dự kiến. Rủi ro: Chi phí vận hành cao hơn dự kiến của các hợp đồng khoan dầu quốc tế. Kỳ vọng giá thuê ngày và hiệu suất hoạt động của giàn JU sẽ phục hồi trong 6 tháng cuối năm 2022. Trong 6 tháng đầu năm 2022, giá thuê ngày JU trung bình của PVD là 57.000 USD (+9,3% YoY) và hiệu suất hoạt động là 76%. PVD kỳ vọng ngày thuê ngày JU trung bình đạt 65.000 USD và hiệu suất động động đạt gần 100% trong 6 tháng cuối năm 2022, điều này sẽ hỗ trợ cho sự phục hồi lợi nhuận của PVD trong nửa cuối năm. Những con số này phù hợp với dự báo hiện tại của chúng tôi cho mức giá thuê ngày trung bình của PVD là 61.000 USD (+14,2 % YoY) và hiệu suất hoạt động là 81% vào năm 2022. Triển vọng giá thuê ngày lạc quan vào năm 2023; chi phí hoạt động dự kiến cao hơn. Theo

Context:
H1 2016 P&L not yet reflective of the overall picture GTN released H1 2016 results with revenues up 35.7% and EPS down 82.4% vs H1 2015. H1 2016 is not reflective of either GTN’s full-year results or its underlying business restructuring. In 2016, we are more attentive to the company’s progress in restructuring its acquired agri-food businesses instead of reading too much into its P&L, which contains non-core businesses to be divested. We do not foresee any material change in our target price but upgrade our recommendation from MARKET PERFORM to OUTPERFORM as the stock has retreated 10.4% since our initiation dated 19 July 2016. Figure 1: GTN's H1 2016 results summary Source: GTN, VCSC Consolidation of Vinatea partly contributed to H1 2016 revenue growth of 35.7% vs H1 2015. We note that Vinatea only started to be consolidated into GTN in August 2015. Strong growth in fertilizer sales, which is a non-core business, further fuelled top line growth. On a standalone basis, Vinatea showed improvements with sales up 17.4% vs H1 2015. At NPAT level, Vinatea also broke even in H1 2016 vs a VND7 billion (USD0.3 million) loss in H1 2015. This is driven by a more effective sales process, e.g. bypassing merchants, in dealing with export partners and higher export ASP aided by quality improvement initiatives. Vinatea, which contributed 46.1% to GTN’s H1 2016 gross profit, saw its GPM enhance from 15.5% in H1 2015 to 16.5% in H1 2016. NPAT declined by 63.4% vs H1 2015 due to restructuring costs and lower net financial income. G&A expenses jumped mostly due to the restructuring activities at Vinatea, which has formed its execution team and been building its sales force and distribution system to prepare for the upcoming launch of branded products in late August 2016. Moreover, in H1 2015, GTN recognized VND19.8 billion (USD0.9 million) financial income from non-core divestments vs VND11 billion (USD0.5 million) in H1 2016. EPS dropped at a steeper rate of 82.4% as GTN carried out a 75mn share private placement in January 2016. This private placement effectively doubled its charter capital from VND750 billion (USD33 million) to VND1,500 billion (67 million). Vinatea’s branded product launch and greater export sales as well as consolidation of Vilico will boost H2 2016. Firstly, Vinatea has signed export contracts totalling USD9 million in the first six months of 2016 but only 52% of that has been recognized. In addition, management aims to secure another USD4.5 million worth of contracts in the second half. Up to now, the total contract value signed is USD10.5 million. Secondly, Vinatea will launch its new branded products for the domestic market in September 2016. Management is targeting VND70-80 billion revenue from these new branded products in FY16, which carry high GPM at around 50%. Finally, Vilico, a dairy business, is set to be consolidated into GTN in Q4 2016. Please see our initiation for more details on Vilico. On a standalone basis, Vilico posted impressive H1 2016 results with NPAT soaring by 50% vs H1 2015 to VND73 billion (USD3 million) thanks to a solid 2.3 percentage point expansion in EBIT margin to 8.9% in H1 2015 as the company improves its procurement of input materials for the dairy business (animal feed, sugar etc.), which led to lower input material costs. Context:
Đặt kế hoạch giảm tỷ lệ sở hữu nhà nước từ năm 2026 28 cổ đông đại diện cho hơn 92% quyền biểu quyết đã tham dự ĐHCĐ của Tập đoàn Bảo Việt (BVH) vào ngày 29/6. Nội dung chính của đại hội nhằm thông qua KQKD năm 2022 cũng như thông qua (1) kế hoạch kinh doanh năm 2023, (2) chiến lược kinh doanh giai đoạn 2021-2030 và (3) kế hoạch cổ tức tiền mặt cho năm 2022. Kế hoạch năm 2023 bao gồm doanh thu của công ty mẹ là 1.53 nghìn tỷ đồng (+1.0% YoY) và LNST là 1.1 nghìn tỷ đồng (+3.2% YoY). Kế hoạch kinh doanh hợp nhất năm 2023 không được đưa vào đề xuất tại ĐHCĐ. ĐHCĐ đã thông qua mức cổ tức bằng tiền mặt 9.54% trên mệnh giá cho năm 2022, tương ứng 954 đồng/cổ phiếu và lợi suất cổ tức là 2.1% (dựa trên giá cổ phiếu hiện tại) — cao hơn kỳ vọng của chúng tôi là 8% mệnh giá. Phần hỏi đáp tập trung vào kết quả hoạt động của BVH trong 6 tháng đầu năm 2023 (6T 2023) cũng như triển vọng năm 2023 và chiến lược kinh doanh dài hạn của tập đoàn. KQKD sơ bộ nửa đầu năm 2023 phù hợp với kỳ vọng của chúng tôi. Trong phần hỏi đáp, ban lãnh đạo cho biết KQKD 6T 2023 đang đi đúng hướng để hoàn thành các chỉ tiêu do ĐHCĐ đề ra. LNST hợp nhất 6T 2023 đạt 866 tỷ đồng (+4.6% YoY; hoàn thành 52.5% kế hoạch kinh doanh) và LNST 6T 2023 công ty mẹ đạt 550 tỷ đồng (+4.6% YoY; hoàn thành 50.1% kế hoạch ĐHĐCĐ). Chúng tôi ước tính rằng KQKD hợp nhất 6T 2023 đã hoàn thành khoảng 45% dự báo cả năm, phù hợp với kỳ vọng của chúng tôi. Nhìn chung theo quan điểm của chúng tôi, mục tiêu kinh doanh của BVH cho công ty mẹ là thận trọng. Phí bảo hiểm nhân thọ phục hồi trong nửa cuối năm 2023. BVH cho biết (1) nhu cầu bảo hiểm suy yếu do việc cắt giảm chi tiêu cá nhân và (2) tâm lý tiêu cực từ các trường hợp vi phạm nguyên tắc bán bancassurance gần đây dẫn đến phí bảo hiểm mới trên toàn hệ thống giảm khoảng 25% YoY trong 5 tháng đầu năm 2023. BVH cho rằng phí bảo hiểm nhân thọ có thể cải thiện trong nửa cuối năm 2023 khi điều kiện kinh tế dần cải thiện. Ngoài ra, các công ty bảo hiểm sẽ phải rút kinh nghiệm từ các trường hợp vi phạm nguyên tắc bán bảo hiểm gần đây và cải thiện dịch vụ bán hàng để đảm bảo tăng trưởng bền vững trong tương lai. Thu nhập đầu tư năm 2023 cải thiện YoY do lãi suất tăng vào cuối năm 2022. Ban lãnh đạo duy trì quan điểm tích cực về tăng trưởng thu nhập đầu tư trong nửa cuối năm 2023 vì hầu hết các khoản đầu tư hiện tại của BVH vẫn đang hưởng lợi suất cao hơn so với cùng kỳ năm ngoái. Ngân hàng Nhà nước Việt Nam (NHNN) gần đây đã giảm lãi suất gần bằng với mức tăng lãi suất vào năm 2022, trong bối cảnh đó, BVH cho biết công ty có thể xem xét tái cơ cấu danh mục đầu tư để chuyển sang trái phiếu doanh nghiệp do các ngân hàng thương mại phát hành nhằm tối đa hóa lợi nhuận. Context:
Tentative EGM in December; conservative Q4 volume We attended DPM’s analyst meeting on November 4. The meeting discussed 9M 2022 results, Q4 2022 sales volume guidance and DPM’s tentative EGM. Below are our key takeaways. DPM plans to hold an EGM in December 2022 to obtain shareholder opinion on 1) payment for a historical extra input gas transportation cost and 2) a proposal for an upward adjustment for the 2022 dividend. DPM recorded payment for extra input gas transportation cost in Q2 2022 results. This amount is USD18.09mn (VND420bn) for the 2014-2018 period and paid to PetroVietnam Gas (HOSE: GAS). This issue was previously received the disapproval of shareholders, as mentioned in our June 23, 2022 DPM AGM Note. DPM plans to raise its 2022 dividend. At DPM’s 2022 AGM, shareholders approved a 2022 dividend of VND5,000/share (12.2% yield) based on the 2022 guidance (see our June 23, 2022 DPM AGM Note). As 9M 2022 NPAT surpassed DPM’s full-year guidance by 27.8%, DPM plans to increase its 2022 dividend. Input gas supply in 2023. DPM shared that in 2023-2025, if gas volume from the Bach Ho cheap gas field as well as expensive gas fields from the Cuu Long basin does not meet its demand, then PVN will allow DPM to source gas from gas fields in the Nam Con Son basin. We assume DPM’s weighted average transportation tariff will increase 22% YoY in 2023F as we expect the company will have to use more gas from expensive gas fields in the Cuu Long basin. 9M 2022 urea average selling price (ASP) and urea sales volume were higher than expected, but input gas costs were lower than expected. 9M 2022 average urea and NPK prices were ~VND15,600/kg (USD627/tonne) and ~VND15,200/kg (USD612/tonne), increasing 67.5% YoY and 65.9% YoY, respectively. The average input gas cost in 9M 2022 was USD8.8/MMBTU (+28.8% YoY). Meanwhile, 9M 2022 volume urea amounted to 641,000 tonnes (+19.2% YoY) — of which one-fourth was export volume. Urea sales sales volume was 114,000 tonnes (-7.0% YoY) in 9M 2022. 9M 2022 urea and NPK sales volume completed 76.3% and 65.4% of our respective full-year forecasts. Overall, we foresee potential upside to our 2022 NPAT forecasts. DPM announced conservative Q4 2022 sales volume guidance despite the expected peak season. DPM guides for urea sales volume of 170,698 tonnes with urea export volume of ~ 55,000 tonnes. In terms of the export market, DPM signed a contract to export 35,000 tonnes of urea mainly to India in Q4 2022. There are some tenders calling for exported urea in Q4 2022, but DPM has not finalized these as the bid prices are low and as it is negotiating more favorable prices. For the domestic market, DPM expects demand to peak in in the winter and spring in the Mekong Delta as well as in northern Vietnam. In addition, DPM expects urea ASP to rise at the end of 2022-January 2023 due to higher demand at the year-end season. DPM guides for a one-month maintenance period in Q2 2023. The company’s CEO states that it normally takes one month for DPM to conduct periodic maintenance every two years. As such, DPM guides for production volume in 2023 to be 70,000 tonnes lower than in 2022. No new information regarding transportation tariff mechanism in 2023 and the 2023 outlook. Despite being asked questions regarding the transportation tariff and 2023 outlook, DPM has not yet provided a clear response. More details will be announced at the company’s EGM.

Context:
Power & Water: Growth in renewable energy Power sector outlook We maintain our optimistic view on Vietnam’s power consumption growth that is driven by the country’s strong economic growth. We expect the Government will raise retail electricity prices ~10% next year due to high gas prices and international coal prices. We expect Vietnam’s Power Development Plan (PDP) VIII will be approved in 2023. As a result, the renewable power sector should expect a new pricing policy that will nearly triple its capacity to 50,000 MW by 2030, in turn making renewable energy Vietnam’s largest source of power. On November 20, 2022, EVN proposed new prices for renewable power that are ~15%-20% lower than the previous favorable feed-in tariffs (FiTs). ​Furthermore, the International Renewable Energy Agency expects average global wind and solar capex per MW to decline ~15%-30% over the next 10 years. In our view, these lower investment costs will help to maintain the profitability of new projects with IRRs of 10%-12%. Just Energy Transition Partnership (JETP) created a turning point for Vietnam’s renewable energy transition. On December 14, 2022, the Vietnamese Government and international partners agreed on a JETP with USD15.5bn of financing over the next five years to support Vietnam’s transition to green energy. We estimate this amount guarantees ~25% of Vietnam’s power capex requirement over the period. We project electricity consumption to rise ~6%/8% YoY in 2022/2023 as Vietnam’s economy recovers from COVID-19. Furthermore, industry players expect Vietnam will enter a slight electricity shortage in 2023, which is partly due to limited capacity growth.​ We forecast competitive generation market (CGM) prices will continue to be strong in 2023 (+9% YoY) and outweigh the negative impact of higher gas prices (+30% YoY) and coal prices (+10% YoY). Renewable power stocks have ample potential for capacity growth — HDG is our top pick. HDG is confident that its An Phong wind power project will be added to PDP VIII. We forecast HDG’s EPS CAGR at 30% in 2021-2026F, and it trades at the cheapest 2023F P/E of 6.4x in our coverage. HDG also trades at 60% discount vs real estate peers with nominal loan & bond financing for its real estate segment. Our second top pick is PC1, which is a renewable power investor and has a power construction segment that secured a record high backlog of VND8tn in 2022. We like also REE as it has the strongest financial health among renewable power stocks to pursue additional green energy capacity expansion.​ Dividends from thermal plants to be supported by earnings recoveries. We believe PPC – our third top pick – will have 2023F NPAT to recover by 50% and offer an increasing yield.​ Additionally, NT2 is poised to deliver a higher dividend (6%-8%) as it is debt free. POW is a play on Vietnam’s structural transition to LNG. POW’s Nhon Trach 3 & 4 plants that are targeted to come online by late 2025 and 2026 will expand its capacity by 35%. ​ Water sector outlook ​ We expect the recent water tariff increase in Binh Phuoc Province will increase the chance for BWE and TDM (our fourth top pick) to receive higher water tariffs starting in 2024. In addition, the water business in Dong Nai Province has great potential with Gia Tan Water JSC and the Long Thanh Highway water project, which could help BWE & TDM to double their capacity in the next 10 years. We like TDM, which has a 37% stake in BWE and projected NPAT 50% YoY growth in 2023. Figure 1: Power & water sector key data as of December 30, 2022

Context:
44% nhu cầu toàn cầu về hạt nhựa PVC – nguyên liệu đầu vào chính của BMP. BMP vẫn là lựa chọn phòng thủ mà chúng tôi đánh giá cao trong ngành xây dựng và vật liệu trong nửa cuối năm 2024. Được hỗ trợ bởi lợi nhuận cao trong năm 2024, nguồn tiền mặt dồi dào và bảng cân đối kế toán ròng tiền mặt, chúng tôi kỳ vọng cổ tức 12 tháng tiếp theo của BMP là 10.800 đồng, tương đương với lợi suất cổ tức ở mức cao là 10,8%. Ngoài ra, BMP là một cổ phiếu có hệ số beta thấp, với hệ số beta điều chỉnh trong 5 và 2 năm của Bloomberg ở mức ~0,85, chúng tôi cho rằng BMP là một lựa chọn phòng thủ hấp dẫn, tận trọng với những biến động và bất ổn hiện tại trên thị trường. Với lợi suất cổ tức bằng tiền mặt cao, chúng tôi tin rằng đợt giảm giá cổ phiếu gần đây đã khiến định giá của BMP trở nên hấp dẫn, với P/E trung bình giai đoạn 2024-25 là 9,2x. Giá mục tiêu của chúng tôi tương ứng với P/E trung bình giai đoạn 2024-25 của BMP ở mức 10,1x, vẫn thấp hơn P/E trung bình 5/10 năm là 10,9x/11,1x. 336 Tổng quan Công ty Thành lập năm 1977, BMP là doanh nghiệp đi tiên phong ngành ống nhựa của Việt Nam. Nawaplastic – một công ty con của Tập đoàn SCG (Thailand) đang nắm cổ phần kiểm soát tại BMP với 54,4% cổ phần. BMP – Sản lượng giảm nhưng biên lợi nhuận vẫn tốt hơn dự kiến Doanh số sẽ phục hồi 17% YoY trong năm 2024. Doanh số bán của NKG giảm 19% YoY trong năm 2023, do giá bán trung bình giảm 18% YoY và sản lượng giảm 2% YoY xuống 856.800 tấn do nhu cầu VLXD thấp. Chúng tôi kỳ vọng mức tăng 17% YoY trong năm 2024, nhờ sản lượng phục hồi 11% YoY và giá bán trung bình tăng 5% nhờ nhu cầu trong nước và xuất khẩu tăng trưởng thấp. Biên lợi nhuận từ HĐKD sẽ phục hồi 160 điểm cơ bản. Chúng tôi kỳ vọng biên lợi nhuận từ HĐKD sẽ tăng 160 điểm cơ bản YoY lên 3,6% trong năm 2024 từ mức thấp 2,0% của năm 2023, nhờ (1) biên lợi nhuận gộp tăng 270 điểm cơ bản, trở lại mức 8,7% từ mức thấp 6,0% của năm 2023 vì chúng tôi kỳ vọng giá bán trung bình toàn ngành sẽ phục hồi nhanh hơn so với giá HRC đầu vào và (2) chi phí SG&A/doanh thu tăng 110 điểm cơ bản lên 5,1% từ mức thấp 4,0% trong năm 2023, khi chúng tôi giả định chi phí vận tải mảng xuất khẩu cao hơn do xung đột hiện tại ở Biển Đỏ và hạn hán nghiêm trọng tại kênh đào Panama. Chúng tôi đưa nhà máy Phú Mỹ của NKG vào mô hình định giá. Cho đến nay, NKG đã đầu tư 500 tỷ đồng trong tổng vốn XDCB là 4,5 nghìn tỷ đồng. Chúng tôi dự kiến ​​nguồn vốn bổ sung là 1,1 nghìn tỷ đồng từ phát hành quyền vào giữa năm 2024 và 3 nghìn tỷ đồng từ nợ vay, và quá trình sản xuất sẽ bắt đầu vào cuối quý 1/2026. Chúng tôi cho rằng mức định giá hiện tại của NKG là không hấp dẫn. Giá cổ phiếu của NKG đã tăng trong 15 tháng qua, chúng tôi cho rằng do thị trường đánh giá cao

Context:
Pharmacy profitability beats our expectations FRT released its 2022 results, including revenue of VND30.2tn (USD1.3bn; +34% YoY) and NPAT-MI of VND390bn (USD16mn; -12% YoY), completing 110% and 107% of our respective full-year forecasts. As such, we see potential upside risk to our projections for FRT, pending a more extensive review. In Q4 2022, FRT’s revenue was VND8.5tn (USD345mn; +0% YoY) with NPAT-MI of VND95bn (USD4mn; -72% YoY). Electronic retail chain FPT Shop’s revenue declined 22% YoY in Q4 2022, which was primarily driven by the global supply shortage of iPhones stemming from China’s commitment to its zero-COVID policy coupled with weak demand for laptops, while Q4 2021 was the high base of iPhones and laptop sales. In 2022, FPT Shop’s revenue advanced 11% YoY. Online sales increased 15% YoY to VND5.7tn (USD240mn), accounting for 27% of FPT Shop’s revenue in 2022. Long Chau’s revenue surged 104% YoY in Q4 2022 and 141% YoY in 2022, which we mainly attribute to the rapid store expansion (its store count was increased 6.4x YoY to 937 as YE2022). Per our estimate, Long Chau’s average monthly revenue/store increased from VND920mn in Q1 2021 and VND1bn in Q2 2021 to nearly VND1.5bn in Q1 2022. It then eased to nearly VND1.0bn in Q2 2022, normalized at ~VND1.1bn in Q3 2022 and inched up to ~VND1.2bn in Q4 2022. Long Chau’s net margin edged up 40 bps YoY to 0.5% in 2022, which was driven by a 3-ppt YoY increase in gross margin and a 2-ppt YoY decrease in SG&A/sales. Nevertheless, FRT’s net margin dropped to 1.3% in 2022 from 2.0% 2021. We believe this result was due to FPT Shop’s lower net profit and net margin, which were partly offset by 83% YoY growth in Long Chau’s NPAT-MI. At YE2022, FRT had 786 FPT Shop stores (vs 647 at YE2021) and 937 Long Chau stores (vs 400 at YE2021) — both of which were ahead the company store opening plan for 2022. Figure 1: FRT’s Q4 2022 and 2022 results Source: FRT, VCSC. Note: * Online sales are included in FPT Shop’s revenue.
